task_completion.description: Open the TurtleBlocks or TurtleArt application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TurtleBlocks/TurtleArt application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Place movement and drawing blocks into the workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Turtle blocks such as 'forward', 'right', 'left', or 'repeat' are present in the main programming workspace.

task_completion.description: Connect the blocks to form a logical sequence for drawing a shape.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The blocks in the workspace are snapped together in a sequence, such as movement and turn blocks placed inside a 'repeat' loop or chained consecutively.
Lines are drawn on the canvas area, and the turtle sprite has moved from its starting position, indicating the program is running or has run.

task_completion.description: The program successfully draws a geometric shape on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clear geometric shape (e.g., square, triangle, polygon, or star pattern) is fully drawn by the turtle on the canvas.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual TurtleBlocks GUI to display the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawn shape and blocks are inside the TurtleBlocks application window, not in an image viewer, web browser, or text editor.

integrity.description: The shape was genuinely drawn by the turtle engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The turtle sprite is visible on the canvas at the end of the drawn lines, consistent with the execution of the block program shown.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by just generating an image via terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of the TurtleBlocks interface with the block code visible alongside the drawn shape.
